channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

Zimbabwe retain core of T20 World Cup 2026 side

(via Three Men On Boat) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Three Men On Boat

item.title: UK Police Name Zimbabwe-Born British Citizen Sought Over Suspected Triple Murder
item.description: LONDON – British police have identified a Zimbabwe-born British citizen as the prime suspect in the alleged murder of his wife and two young daughters after their bodies were discovered at their home in Great Denham, prompting an international manhunt. According to Sky News, Ndodana Mkhanyisi Tshuma, 45, who is also known as Mark, is [...] The post UK Police Name Zimbabwe-Born British Citizen Sought Over Suspected Triple Murder appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: IMF Backs Zimbabwe’s Reform Progress as Staff-Level Deal Boosts Debt Clearance and Currency Reform Agenda
item.description: HARARE – Zimbabwe has moved a step closer to restoring relations with international financial institutions after the International Monetary Fund reached a staff-level agreement with the government on the first review of its 10-month Staff-Monitored Programme (SMP), signalling growing confidence in the country’s macroeconomic stabilisation efforts and reform agenda. The agreement, which remains subject to [...] The post IMF Backs Zimbabwe’s Reform Progress as Staff-Level Deal Boosts Debt Clearance and Currency Reform Agenda appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: Private Hospitals Barred From Refusing Emergency Patients Regardless of Ability to Pay
item.description: Private Hospitals Barred From Refusing Emergency Patients Under New Law Private hospitals and other health institutions in Zimbabwe will now be legally required to admit and treat patients with life-threatening medical conditions, regardless of whether they can afford to pay, following changes to the Medical Services Act. The amendments, published in the Government Gazette on [...] The post Private Hospitals Barred From Refusing Emergency Patients Regardless of Ability to Pay appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.title: UK: Woman And 2 Kids Found Dead
item.description: By A Correspondent | ZimEye | Bedfordshire Police have released a CCTV image of a man wanted in connection with... Continue reading "UK: Woman And 2 Kids Found Dead" (via Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwe News

item.title: UK police launch international manhunt for suspect believed to have fled to Zimbabwe after murdering wife, two daughters
item.description: Agencies A Zimbabwean-born man is the subject of an international manhunt after police named him as the prime suspect in the alleged murder of his wife and two young daughters at their home in Bedfordshire, before he is believed to have fled to Zimbabwe. Bedfordshire Police identified the suspect as Ndodana Mkhanyisi Tshuma, 45, who [...] The post UK police launch international manhunt for suspect believed to have fled to Zimbabwe after murdering wife, two daughters appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: From Constitutional Consolidation to Developmental Statecraft: Reframing Zimbabwe’s Post-CAB3 Political Economy
item.description: The End of Constitutional Politics and the Beginning of Governance: The enactment of the Constitutional Amendment Bill marks the conclusion of one of the most consequential periods of constitutional politics in Zimbabwe since the adoption of the 2013 Constitution. By Brighton Musonza Whether the amendments are viewed as institutional refinement or political consolidation is now [...] The post From Constitutional Consolidation to Developmental Statecraft: Reframing Zimbabwe’s Post-CAB3 Political Economy appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: Etihad Airways, Fastjet Zimbabwe Forge Strategic Alliance to Expand Regional and Global Connectivity
item.description: HARARE – Etihad Airways and Fastjet Zimbabwe have entered into a strategic partnership that will allow passengers to travel seamlessly between the two airlines’ networks, strengthening Zimbabwe’s international air connectivity and supporting the country’s tourism and business sectors. The agreement, signed in Harare on Tuesday, provides for an interline partnership, a future codeshare arrangement and [...] The post Etihad Airways, Fastjet Zimbabwe Forge Strategic Alliance to Expand Regional and Global Connectivity appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: Namib Minerals makes new board and executive appointments
item.description: Gold and exploration company Namib Minerals has announced a series of board and executive leadership appointments to strengthen oversight and reinforce financial leadership to support the company’s next phase of growth, including the planned restart of the Redwing mine, in Zimbabwe, and the execution of its development financing strategy. In addition to serving the company as CEO, Tulani Sikwila has been appointed as chairperson, while businessperson Wendy Luhabe has been appointed independent nonexecutive director and lead independent director and Sphe Mchunu CFO. (via Australasia Global Mining News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Australasia Global Mining News

item.title: The 16-Year Presidency: The Mathematical Reality of Why You Won’t Vote Until Year 2044
item.description: On a quiet Tuesday of 7 July 2026, while most Zimbabweans were preoccupied with the soaring price of basic commodities and the daily struggle for transport, a single stroke of a pen altered the course of the nation’s history. President Emmerson Mnangagwa, the man who referred to himself as a “constitutionalist” and a “listening leader,” [...] The post The 16-Year Presidency: The Mathematical Reality of Why You Won’t Vote Until Year 2044 first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: The Mercenary Trap: How a Hwange ‘Job Agent’ Sold Zimbabweans to the Frontlines of Russia
item.description: HWANGE — In a chilling investigation that reads like a thriller, Oscar Sifelani Mtshiya, a 48-year-old man from Hwange, recently appeared in court facing human trafficking charges. He didn’t just lie to job seekers; he allegedly sold them into a war zone. Lured with the promise of high-paying firefighting jobs in Moscow, five Zimbabweans found [...] The post The Mercenary Trap: How a Hwange ‘Job Agent’ Sold Zimbabweans to the Frontlines of Russia first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: Goodbye Chiwenga, succession problem SOLVED as President Mnangagwa signs CAB 3 into law.... Here is what’s next
item.description: President Mnangagwa signs CAB3 into law, radically reshaping Zimbabwe’s volatile political landscape HARARE — On the evening of 6 July 2026, Zimbabwe was plunged into a profound and literal darkness. A massive technical fault on the Warren-Alaska 330kV transmission line triggered a nationwide grid collapse, silencing the hum of the cities and leaving millions in [...] The post Goodbye Chiwenga, succession problem SOLVED as President Mnangagwa signs CAB 3 into law.... Here is what’s next first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News
